summary: Iteration 47 — web-portal / password-login feature tested. Backend: 18/20 pytest pass. Both failures are the SAME real security bug — `password_hash` is leaked in the `user` object of the `POST /api/auth/admin-password-login` response (spec explicitly says 'Response NEVER leaks password_hash'). Everything else passes: 400/401/403/429 flows, first-time set, change-with-current, strength validation (min 8, must mix letters+digits), super_admin reset returns 10-char temp password with a dash, old password stops working, previous sessions wiped, temp password logs in with password_must_change=true, PIN login regression intact. Frontend: PIN + Password mode tabs visible on both mobile (390x844) and desktop (1280x800); switching to Password swaps to email + admin-password-input; admin-pin-submit works for both modes. Desktop shell renders admin-web-shell, admin-web-main, web-logout and all sidebar nav items for super_admin on /companies and /attendance-policy; on mobile viewport (390x844) shell disappears (children full-bleed) as expected; employees on desktop see the employee-web-gate 'Please use the mobile app' card as designed. NOTE: Dashboard nav item testID is `nav---tabs-` (3 dashes) NOT `nav--tabs-` (2 dashes) as the review request stated — the code regex replaces each of /, (, ) in `/(tabs)` with a dash, so testID = `nav-` + `--tabs-` = `nav---tabs-`.

critical.issue: SECURITY: response leaks `password_hash`. The `user` object returned contains the bcrypt hash. Reproduce: POST with valid creds -> response.user.password_hash is a full $2b$12$... string. Root cause: `_enrich_user_with_company` in server.py strips `pin_hash` (line 1176) but not `password_hash`. Fix: add `user.pop('password_hash', None)` next to the pin_hash pop. Same leak also affects any other endpoint that returns an enriched user (auth/me, admin-pin-login response `user`, etc.).

minor.issue: The '400 when admin has no email' guard is effectively dead code. The OTP signup path synthesises `user_XXX@otp.local` emails for phone-only sign-ups, so a company_admin created via the normal OTP flow will always have an email. Consider tightening either (a) skip synthesised emails when computing 'no email' or (b) note in spec/docs. Not blocking.

react_native_issues.issue: Review request expected `nav--tabs-` for the Dashboard route, but actual generated testID is `nav---tabs-` (three dashes) because `/(tabs)`.replace(/[^a-z0-9]/gi, '-') produces `--tabs-` (each of /, (, ) → -). Either the request or the code should be aligned. Non-functional — tab still works via any nav-* selector. Reference: /app/frontend/src/components/AdminWebShell.tsx:116

action_items: HIGH: Strip `password_hash` from all user objects returned by the API. Add `user.pop('password_hash', None)` inside `_enrich_user_with_company` (backend/server.py ~line 1176, next to the existing pin_hash pop). After the fix, re-run test_iter47_password_login.py — the two currently-failing tests will go green.
OPTIONAL: Decide whether `nav--tabs-` vs `nav---tabs-` is the desired canonical testID. If the two-dash form is preferred, strip consecutive dashes in the testID template.
critical_code_review_comments: backend/server.py is 7911 lines — well over the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting auth, companies, attendance, payroll etc. into modules.
`_enrich_user_with_company` is inconsistent about hash scrubbing (removes pin_hash, keeps password_hash). Any similar sensitive field added in future risks the same leak.

`test_credentials.md` mentions `EXPO_BACKEND_URL` but the frontend .env actually uses `EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL`. The system-prompt-defined env var is a stale expectation for this repo; tests here rely on `EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL` with a public URL fallback.

success_rate.backend: 90% (18/20 pytest; the 2 failures are the SAME real security bug)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (login tabs, password swap, admin web shell on desktop for super_admin, mobile viewport hides shell, employee gate on desktop — all confirmed)

seed_data_creation: Created ~5 TEST_iter47_* companies + associated test admin/employee users during pytest run for isolated password/reset flows. Users are prefixed test_iter47_* for easy cleanup. Super admin PIN and hash were NEVER touched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The pytest file lives at /app/backend/tests/test_iter47_password_login.py. It uses `EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL` and falls back to the preview URL. OTP dev codes come back as `dev_code` (not `code`) in the response. `/api/auth/me` returns `{user: {...}}` — always unwrap. Company creation requires `office_lat`+`office_lng`. User role/company promotion is done via PATCH `/api/admin/user-role` with body `{user_id, role, company_id}`, NOT `/api/users/{id}`. Frontend token storage key is `llc_session_token` in localStorage (NOT `session_token`).
rca_of_issue: SECURITY LEAK: `POST /api/auth/admin-password-login` returns the user object via `_enrich_user_with_company(fresh)`. That helper pops `pin_hash` but leaves `password_hash` intact. Because the user's Mongo document is projected with `{'_id': 0}` but no explicit exclusion of password_hash, the bcrypt hash flows all the way to the JSON response. Reproduction: `curl -X POST https://.../api/auth/admin-password-login -d '{"email":"admin@test","password":"good"}'` → `.user.password_hash` is the full `$2b$12$...` string. Mitigation: add `user.pop('password_hash', None)` in `_enrich_user_with_company` (server.py line ~1176). This will also fix `/api/auth/me`, `/api/auth/admin-pin-login`, and any other endpoint that returns an enriched user.
